skill.name: prd
skill.description: Generate high-quality Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for software systems and AI-powered features. Includes executive summaries, user stories, technical specifications, and risk analysis.
skill.summary: Create structured PRDs with discovery questions, requirements, user stories, technical specifications, risks, and rollout plans.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections are false positives in Markdown prose and examples. The skill contains no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection language.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backtick locations are code fences, inline examples, or PRD tool names, not executable Ruby or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a Markdown instruction document. The flagged text is not executable code and does not pass user input to a command interpreter.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Detections Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines do not configure cryptographic algorithms or implement cryptographic behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic API, cipher selection, hashing routine, or security-sensitive implementation is present. The matches occur in documentation text only.

low_findings.title: Static System Reconnaissance Detection Is Requirements Guidance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line tells writers to use measurable requirements and does not gather host or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain PRD writing guidance. There is no command, filesystem access, network request, or instruction to enumerate system data.

content.value_statement: Teams often start features with unclear goals, missing constraints, and weak acceptance criteria. This skill turns product ideas into structured PRDs with discovery questions, measurable requirements, and technical planning sections.
actual_capabilities: Guides discovery with questions about problems, metrics, constraints, and scope.
Produces PRDs with executive summaries, user stories, acceptance criteria, and non-goals.
Adds AI system requirements when the feature includes AI behavior.
Documents architecture, integrations, security, privacy, risks, and rollout plans.
Improves vague requirements by converting them into measurable acceptance criteria.
limitations: It does not validate business assumptions without user input or external research.
It does not create implementation code or project tickets by itself.
It needs product context, constraints, and stakeholder goals for strong output.
It does not replace legal, privacy, or security review for regulated products.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn an early product idea into a complete PRD with goals, user stories, acceptance criteria, and rollout risks.

use_cases.title: Define AI Feature Requirements
use_cases.description: Document AI tool needs, quality evaluation, security expectations, and success metrics before engineering starts.

use_cases.title: Align Engineering Scope
use_cases.description: Create a shared specification that clarifies integrations, non-goals, technical risks, and phased delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a PRD for a feature that lets users save favorite reports. Ask clarifying questions before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Notes into a PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Use rough planning notes as input.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these notes into a structured PRD. Identify missing assumptions, non-goals, metrics, and acceptance criteria: [paste notes].

prompt_templates.title: Plan an AI Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PRD for an AI assistant that answers customer support questions. Include evaluation strategy, safety requirements, and rollout risks.

prompt_templates.title: Review and Strengthen a PRD
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing requirements document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PRD for vague requirements, missing technical constraints, weak metrics, and unclear risks. Then propose a stronger version: [paste PRD].

output_examples.input: A team wants a better document search experience for developers.
output_examples.output: A PRD with problem statement, success metrics, developer personas, search user stories, acceptance criteria, architecture notes, and rollout risks.

output_examples.input: A product lead needs requirements for an AI support assistant.
output_examples.output: A structured PRD covering tool needs, answer quality evaluation, privacy controls, human fallback, measurable KPIs, and phased release steps.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead has a vague feature request with no constraints.
output_examples.output: Discovery questions, clarified scope, explicit non-goals, technical assumptions marked as TBD, and measurable acceptance criteria.

best_practices: Provide product goals, target users, constraints, and known metrics before asking for a full PRD.
Review the generated non-goals with stakeholders before development starts.
Ask for measurable acceptance criteria when requirements sound subjective or vague.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final PRD without answering discovery questions.
Do not treat generated technical assumptions as approved architecture decisions.
Do not omit risks, non-goals, or evaluation criteria to make the plan look simpler.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured Product Requirements Documents with goals, user stories, acceptance criteria, technical notes, risks, and rollout plans.

faq.question: Can it write PRDs for AI features?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes AI system requirements, evaluation strategy, safety considerations, and tool or API needs when relevant.

faq.question: Does it ask questions before drafting?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires discovery questions about the problem, success metrics, constraints, and scope.

faq.question: Can it replace a product manager?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure requirements, but stakeholders must validate strategy, priorities, feasibility, and business impact.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access external systems?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown instruction file and does not contain executable scripts or external integrations.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
